task_completion.description: Configure Screaming Frog to send the custom HTTP request header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog's 'Custom HTTP Header' configuration window is visible, with 'X-Audit-Token' entered as the Header Name and 'SF-Verified-9988' as the Header Value.

task_completion.description: Configure Screaming Frog to perform a custom search for the header value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog's 'Custom Search' configuration window is visible, showing a search rule configured to find the text 'SF-Verified-9988'.
The main Screaming Frog interface shows 'https://crawler-test.com/other/crawler_request_headers' entered in the top URL bar, and the crawl progress indicates it has been spidered.

task_completion.description: Verify the header is received by checking the custom search results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Custom Search' tab is active in the main interface of Screaming Frog, showing a successful match for 'SF-Verified-9988' on the target URL.

task_completion.description: Export the custom search results to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the file being saved as 'custom_header_evidence.csv' in the 'Documents/SEO/exports' directory, or a terminal/file manager showing the file exists at '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/custom_header_evidence.csv'.

integrity.description: Verify that Screaming Frog SEO Spider's graphical interface was used to configure the crawl and search.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Screaming Frog application windows and dialogs being interacted with, rather than terminal commands editing configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify that a genuine crawl took place.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog interface shows realistic crawl data such as Status Code 200, Content Type, and response times for the target URL.

integrity.description: Verify the export file was generated by Screaming Frog.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Export' button within the Screaming Frog interface rather than manually creating a CSV file in a text editor or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://crawler-test.com/other/crawler_request_headers. Required Header Name: X-Audit-Token. Required Header Value: SF-Verified-9988. Expected Export Path: /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/custom_header_evidence.csv. The target URL echoes HTTP request headers in its response, so a successful crawl with the custom header will contain 'SF-Verified-9988' in the page source.
